definitions.text: Originally, a stick; now specifically, a long and slender piece of metal or (especially) wood, used for various construction or support purposes.
A construction by which an animal is harnessed to a carriage.
A type of basic fishing rod.
To propel by pushing with poles, to push with a pole.
examples: Huck Finn poled that raft southward down the Mississippi because going northward against the current was too much work.

definitions.text: To identify something quite precisely using a telescope.
examples: He poled off the serial of the Gulfstream to confirm its identity.

definitions.text: To furnish with poles for support.
Either of the two points on the earth's surface around which it rotates; also, similar points on any other rotating object.
A point of magnetic focus, especially each of the two opposing such points of a magnet (designated north and south).
Any of a small set of extremes; especially, either of two extremes that are possible or available.
examples: In discussing alternatives to the polar extremes, Professor Nguyen mentioned two poles of a filthy floor versus a sterile surgical site.

definitions.text: To induce piezoelectricity in (a substance) by aligning the dipoles.
